chunks.text: the price of most goods and services is determined by demand and supply.
When consumers strongly demand a certain product, prices rise.
When demand falls, prices fall as well.
This is the most basic mechanism of the market.
A company gathers capital, conducts production in a factory
and sells its product to gain profit.
In an industry where competition is active, companies are pushed to improve their quality.
Workers provide labor and, in return, receive wage or salary.
When employment is stable, the income of households is also stable.
A nation gathers funds by tax and uses them for the budget of education and welfare.
A bank provides loan, and companies grow through investment.
In the modern era, where globalization has advanced
the economy of one country is closely tied to the markets of others.
Economies, beyond the borders of states, continually influence one another.
